microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces or cold items THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user likely wants a normally warm meal without extra delay. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated entree foods THEN 3 min BECAUSE fatigue makes comfort more important and hotter food can feel more satisfying. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND any hot entree foods THEN 1 min BECAUSE this user tolerates slightly less heating to reduce total meal time. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND standard hot entree foods THEN 2 min BECAUSE social context does not strongly change heating preference. IF watching television or working on a laptop in front AND relaxed with ample time AND refrigerated entree foods THEN 3 min BECAUSE distraction plus extra time favors a hotter meal before eating begins. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND fruit or cold dessert foods THEN no microwave BECAUSE those foods are typically preferred cold. IF any dining context AND neutral or relaxed AND brownie or warm dessert foods THEN 1 min BECAUSE a light reheat improves palatability without overheating dessert.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE there is no competing visual target and efficiency matters more. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any foods THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE preserving the front view matters more than shortest motion. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any foods THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE this user still prioritizes keeping the screen visible over speed when a task is in front. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left AND neutral AND any foods THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user likely wants to keep that partner visible. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the right AND neutral AND any foods THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE maintaining right-side social visibility is preferred. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND any foods THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE under fatigue this user prioritizes lower effort and simpler robot behavior. IF watching television in front or working on a laptop in front AND physically fatigued AND messy or sauce foods THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE distraction remains the tie-breaker even when fatigue would otherwise favor simpler motions.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN medium BECAUSE the user prefers balanced efficiency and comfort. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND any foods THEN slow BECAUSE reduced neck and trunk control make gentler motion more comfortable. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND easy-to-acquire foods like meats or brownies THEN fast BECAUSE this user accepts more automation and speed when trying to finish sooner. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any foods THEN medium BECAUSE social comfort argues against fast motion but the user is not highly cautious. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND physically fatigued AND any foods THEN slow BECAUSE fatigue is the tie-breaker and comfort overrides efficiency. IF watching television or working on a laptop in front AND neutral AND any foods THEN medium BECAUSE distraction suggests avoiding abrupt motion but not necessarily slowing to the minimum. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND messy foods with sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE slower motion reduces spill risk and the user is not time-pressured.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND bite-sized foods without sauces THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes narrower bites easier to eat. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND larger or tougher foods like chicken or beef THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE easier oral transfer matters more than maximum acquisition success. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND slippery foods or foods with repeated acquisition failures THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this user will trade bite narrowness for more reliable pickup when speed matters. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND foods prone to failed acquisition THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE avoiding repeated failed attempts is socially preferable. IF watching television or working on a laptop in front AND neutral AND standard easy foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the default comfort preference remains. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND soft foods that can be acquired either way THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE comfort dominates when there is no time pressure. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND desserts or soft fruits with unstable geometry THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE efficient successful pickup becomes the tie-breaker.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN yes BECAUSE the user can operate the web interface and prefers reliable transfers over occasional empty-fork attempts. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND easy-to-acquire foods THEN no BECAUSE this user will skip confirmation to reduce interaction time when failure risk is low. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any foods THEN yes BECAUSE explicit confirmation avoids awkward repeated failed transfers. IF watching television or working on a laptop in front AND neutral AND any foods THEN no BECAUSE extra interface steps interrupt the primary activity. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND easy foods with high acquisition success THEN no BECAUSE fatigue shifts preference toward lower-effort interaction. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND messy foods or foods with frequent acquisition failures THEN yes BECAUSE avoiding a bad transfer outweighs the effort of confirming. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND difficult foods like slippery fruit or sauced items THEN yes BECAUSE the user is willing to spend time to ensure success.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any foods THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward reliably due to limited trunk control. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any foods THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE physical constraints override any social desire for more distance. IF watching television or working on a laptop in front AND neutral AND any foods THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE maintaining posture while multitasking makes outside-mouth transfer impractical. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any foods THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE fatigue further reduces ability to move toward the utensil. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any foods THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE it is the most direct and physically compatible method. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND any foods THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE extra time does not change the user’s inability to lean forward. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND messy or sauce foods THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE closer controlled placement reduces the need for user movement and helps manage the bite.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any foods THEN not applicable BECAUSE the default transfer mode is inside mouth transfer. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any foods THEN not applicable BECAUSE social context does not overcome the user’s trunk limitations. IF watching television or working on a laptop in front AND neutral AND any foods THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user still needs inside-mouth transfer. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any foods THEN not applicable BECAUSE fatigue strengthens the need for inside-mouth transfer. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any foods THEN not applicable BECAUSE faster service still relies on inside-mouth transfer. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND any foods THEN not applicable BECAUSE additional time does not make outside-mouth transfer feasible. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND messy or sauce foods THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user’s stable physical needs keep this dimension unused.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN speech + LED BECAUSE multimodal cues are clear without requiring extra effort. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any foods THEN LED BECAUSE a quieter cue is less disruptive to conversation while still visible. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any foods THEN LED BECAUSE speech may compete with audio from the TV. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any foods THEN LED BECAUSE visual notification is sufficient and less interruptive. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any foods THEN speech + LED BECAUSE fatigue increases the value of redundant cues that reduce missed prompts. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any foods THEN LED BECAUSE the user prefers a brief low-friction cue when moving quickly through the meal. IF eating in a social setting AND physically fatigued AND any foods THEN speech + LED BECAUSE this user’s tie-breaker under fatigue is reducing effort and missed signals, even if speech is slightly more noticeable.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN button BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes open-mouth detection unreliable and the user can press buttons. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any foods THEN button BECAUSE explicit control avoids confusion with talking and fits the user’s abilities. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any foods THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue is the tie-breaker and reduces willingness to press repeatedly. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any foods THEN button BECAUSE the user may be distracted, so explicit initiation is safer than automatic motion. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any foods THEN autocontinue BECAUSE efficiency plus divided attention makes automatic progression preferable. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND difficult foods THEN button BECAUSE the user prefers deliberate control when not rushed. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any foods THEN never choose open mouth BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes that interaction channel unreliable.

IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any drinks THEN button BECAUSE the user can reliably press a button and open-mouth readiness is difficult. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any drinks THEN button BECAUSE explicit signaling avoids ambiguity during conversation. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any drinks THEN autocontinue BECAUSE reducing repeated manual input becomes more important under fatigue. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any drinks THEN button BECAUSE distraction makes explicit initiation preferable to unexpected robot motion. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any drinks THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user prioritizes efficiency when multitasking. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND any drinks THEN button BECAUSE deliberate control matches the user’s stable interaction style when effort is manageable. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any drinks THEN never choose open mouth BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes it a poor readiness signal.

IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND no messy foods THEN button BECAUSE the user prefers explicit control and can press a button. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any wiping need THEN button BECAUSE explicit initiation avoids awkward mistimed wiping. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND messy or sauce foods THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue plus immediate need for wiping favors lower-effort automation. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any wiping need THEN button BECAUSE the user may be distracted and wants to control timing. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any wiping need THEN autocontinue BECAUSE efficiency and divided attention outweigh manual control. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND any wiping need THEN button BECAUSE the user is comfortable manually initiating when not fatigued. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any wiping need THEN never choose open mouth BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes the signal unreliable.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN speech + LED BECAUSE redundant cues clearly indicate when to take the bite or sip. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any foods or drinks THEN LED BECAUSE a quieter cue is less socially disruptive. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any foods or drinks THEN LED BECAUSE speech may interfere with media audio. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any foods or drinks THEN LED BECAUSE visual notification is less interruptive while working. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any transfer type THEN speech + LED BECAUSE fatigue increases the need for unmistakable timing cues. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND any transfer type THEN LED BECAUSE the user prefers a brief cue that does not slow the interaction. IF eating in a social setting AND physically fatigued AND any transfer type THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user’s tie-breaker under fatigue is maximizing clarity even in social contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN perception BECAUSE fork-based sensing is reliable and avoids extra button presses. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any foods THEN perception BECAUSE it is unobtrusive and does not require the user to interrupt conversation. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any foods THEN perception BECAUSE low-effort completion detection is strongly preferred. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any foods THEN perception BECAUSE the user may be distracted and should not need to signal manually. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any foods THEN perception BECAUSE it supports efficient hands-light interaction. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND difficult foods THEN perception BECAUSE reliability still outweighs manual control. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any foods THEN never choose button unless perception fails repeatedly BECAUSE the user can press buttons but prefers not to add unnecessary actions.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any drinks THEN button BECAUSE head-gesture perception is unreliable given limited neck control. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any drinks THEN button BECAUSE explicit completion is more reliable than head gestures and avoids awkward repeated prompts. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any drinks THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue is the tie-breaker and repeated button use becomes burdensome. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any drinks THEN button BECAUSE distraction makes a long automatic wait less desirable than explicit completion. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any drinks THEN autocontinue BECAUSE efficiency and divided attention favor automatic progression. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND any drinks THEN button BECAUSE the user prefers reliable explicit control when effort is manageable. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any drinks THEN never choose perception BECAUSE limited neck control makes head-nod completion unreliable.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any wiping need THEN button BECAUSE head-gesture perception is unreliable and the user can signal explicitly. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any wiping need THEN button BECAUSE explicit completion prevents awkward mistiming. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND messy or sauce foods THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue plus frequent wiping makes manual completion burdensome. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any wiping need THEN button BECAUSE distraction makes explicit completion more predictable. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any wiping need THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user prioritizes lower interaction overhead. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND any wiping need THEN button BECAUSE reliable manual control fits the user’s stable preference. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any wiping need THEN never choose perception BECAUSE limited neck control makes head-based completion detection unsuitable.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN no BECAUSE the user values efficiency when no external visual target is important. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any foods THEN yes BECAUSE preserving the front view matters during the meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any foods THEN yes BECAUSE the user wants the screen unobstructed between actions. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left AND neutral AND any foods THEN yes BECAUSE prolonged obstruction of the partner is undesirable. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the right AND neutral AND any foods THEN yes BECAUSE maintaining social visibility is preferred. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any foods THEN no BECAUSE fatigue is the tie-breaker and shorter simpler robot behavior is preferred. IF watching television or working on a laptop in front AND physically fatigued AND any foods THEN yes BECAUSE preserving the main visual target outweighs the extra time even under fatigue.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE there is nothing to dip and the default should avoid unnecessary mess. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND foods with a preferred sauce or dip THEN more BECAUSE the user can enjoy flavor when there is no social pressure. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND foods with sauce THEN less BECAUSE lighter dipping reduces mess and speeds the meal. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND foods with sauce THEN less BECAUSE the user wants to reduce messiness in front of others. IF watching television or working on a laptop in front AND neutral AND foods with sauce THEN less BECAUSE the user wants to avoid drips while distracted. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND foods with sauce THEN less BECAUSE fatigue increases the value of cleaner easier transfers. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND foods without a dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND foods without sauces THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user prefers a moderate balance between autonomy and time to intervene. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND any foods THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user wants the system to keep moving. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any foods or drinks THEN 10 sec BECAUSE fatigue shifts preference toward lower-effort automation and less waiting. IF eating in a social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND any foods or drinks THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user may be intermittently engaged with conversation and wants some buffer. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any foods or drinks THEN 100 sec BECAUSE distraction argues for a moderate wait rather than immediate continuation. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any foods or drinks THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE task engagement may delay responses and the user wants ample time to intervene. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND in a hurry AND any foods or drinks THEN 100 sec BECAUSE urgency shortens the wait, but not to 10 sec, since multitasking still creates missed-response risk.
